titleStmt.title: SII 40.160: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 160. (A.R. No. B. 294 of 1931.) Jūlakallu, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a slab set up near a well called Peddabhāvi in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1711, Saumya, Mārgaśira, śu. 15.

Records the reconstruction of the temples of Gōpālasvāmi and Bhīmēśvarasvāmi (since last four years it is not under worship) at Jūlakallu in Bellamkoṇḍa-sīma by Buchchannagāru, a kamma of the Kāśikēla-gōtra. Further it records the gift of lands to the above temple for conducting worship and offerings, and also the reconstruction of the temple of Vighnēśvara, repair of a tank and digging of a well in the village during the time of Rājaśrī Malrāju Guṇḍārāyaniṇgāru.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śā
livāhana śakavarṣaṁbulu 1711
Agunēṭi saumya nāma saṁvatsara mārgaśira
śu 15 lu bellaṁkoṁḍḍa śīmalō jūlakaṁṭi grā
māna śrīmadakhilāṁḍakōṭi brahmāṁḍanā
yakulaynigōpālasvāmi bhīmēśvara svā
mivāri staḷālu reṁḍnū pūjalēka nālgu
saṁvatsaramulu khilamaivuṁḍe ganaka
rājaśrīmalrārāju guṁḍārāyaniṁ
gāri prabhutvamulōnu kaṁmmavaṁśaju
laynikāśikēla gōtrōdbhavulunnu paṁggu
lūri rāgaṁnnagāri pautṛlunnu rāmaṁ
nnagāri putṛluṁnaiyni buccaṁnnagāru
saṁprōkṣaṇa tirulyāṇa mahōtsakāla
maṁddu māku dhōravāru śalavu yicina
mānyapu polamulōnu ceri pātika velipo
lamunnu cerinālgu mānikela vittanāla va
ripolaṁ naivēdya dīpārādhanaku samarpistimi
Idigāka vūri tūrpu ceruvu tegi
peddabhāvi mīdugā pārli vināyaku
ḍu Ālayaṁ khilamāyanu nūtana śila vi
ghnēśvara pratiṣṭacestimi nūti pūḍike tiyi
ṁcci ceruvu gaṁḍiveśipai kaṭṭaveyiṁccika
luju kaṭṭiṁcci taṭāka kūpa pratiṣṭa cēśi
vēyiṁcina dharmaśāsanaṁ yī kaiṁkaryaṁ Ācaṁ
drasthāyigānu koṁnavudhariṁccavalenu
maṁggaḷamahāśrīśrīśrīṁjeyanū
svadattā dviguṇaṁ puṁṇyaṁ . paradattānu
pālanaṁ . paradattāpahārēṇa . svadattaṁ niṣpala
ṁbhavēttu .
Digital edition of SII 40.160 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
